meta.geometry_note: All sections MCQ only. Questions may reference figures described in text. Student selects from options — no drawing or typing of geometry answers required.

questions.text: The sum of all interior angles of a quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral divides into 2 triangles. Sum = 2 × 180° = 360°.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, if ∠A = 70°, then ∠C = ?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal. ∠A = ∠C = 70°.

questions.text: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is called a:
questions.explanation: A trapezium has exactly one pair of parallel sides.

questions.text: The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at:

options.d: They do not bisect each other

questions.explanation: In a rhombus, diagonals bisect each other at right angles (90°).

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, ∠A = 70°. What is ∠B?
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary. ∠A + ∠B = 180°. ∠B = 180° − 70° = 110°.

questions.text: The sum of all exterior angles of any convex polygon is:

options.c: Depends on the number of sides

questions.explanation: The sum of all exterior angles of any convex polygon is always 360°.

questions.text: A regular polygon has each exterior angle = 45°. How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 360° ÷ exterior angle = 360° ÷ 45° = 8 sides.

questions.text: The sum of interior angles of a hexagon is:
Each interior angle of a regular pentagon is:
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (5−2) × 180° = 540°. Each angle = 540° ÷ 5 = 108°.

questions.text: A square is a special rectangle because it also has:
questions.explanation: A rectangle already has all angles 90°, equal diagonals, and opposite sides parallel. What makes a square special is that additionally all sides are equal.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a kite with AB = AD and CB = CD. Which diagonal is the axis of symmetry?
questions.explanation: In kite ABCD with AB=AD and CB=CD, diagonal AC is the axis of symmetry. BD is bisected by AC but BD is not the axis of symmetry.

questions.text: In a parallelogram PQRS, PQ = 8 cm and QR = 5 cm. What is PS?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal. PS is opposite to QR, so PS = QR = 5 cm.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a property of a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other but are NOT necessarily equal. Equal diagonals is a property of rectangles, not all parallelograms.

questions.text: The number of sides of a polygon whose sum of interior angles is 1080° is:
In the figure, ABCD is a rhombus. Its diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. If AC = 12 cm and BD = 16 cm, what is AO?
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other. AO = AC/2 = 12/2 = 6 cm.

questions.text: A polygon is said to be regular if:

options.c: All sides and all angles are equal
options.d: It has an even number of sides

questions.explanation: A regular polygon has ALL sides equal AND ALL angles equal.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a trapezium with AB ∥ DC. If ∠A = 80°, then ∠D = ?
questions.explanation: In a trapezium with AB ∥ DC, co-interior angles (same side of transversal) are supplementary. ∠A + ∠D = 180°. ∠D = 180° − 80° = 100°.

questions.text: Which of these quadrilaterals has diagonals that are perpendicular bisectors of each other?
questions.explanation: In a rhombus, the diagonals are perpendicular bisectors of each other (bisect at 90°).

questions.text: How many diagonals does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals = n(n−3)/2 = 6(6−3)/2 = 6×3/2 = 9.

questions.text: In the figure, three angles of a quadrilateral ABCD are ∠A = 90°, ∠B = 80°, ∠C = 100°. What is ∠D?
In parallelogram ABCD, ∠A = (3x + 10)° and ∠C = (5x − 30)°. Find ∠B.
questions.explanation: Opposite angles equal: 3x+10 = 5x−30 → 40 = 2x → x = 20. ∠A = 70°. Adjacent angles supplementary: ∠B = 180° − 70° = 110°.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a rhombus with diagonal AC = 10 cm and BD = 24 cm. What is the side AB?
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect at right angles. AO = 5 cm, BO = 12 cm. AB² = AO² + BO² = 25 + 144 = 169. AB = 13 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, PQRS is a parallelogram. The diagonals PR and QS intersect at O. If PO = 4.5 cm and QO = 3 cm, what are PR and QS?
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. PO = OR = 4.5 cm → PR = 9 cm. QO = OS = 3 cm → QS = 6 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a rectangle with AB = 8 cm and BC = 6 cm. What is the length of diagonal AC?
questions.explanation: In rectangle ABCD, angle B = 90°. AC² = AB² + BC² = 64 + 36 = 100. AC = 10 cm.

questions.text: Three angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 2:3:4. The fourth angle is 90°. What is the largest angle?
questions.explanation: Three angles in ratio 2:3:4. Let them be 2k, 3k, 4k. 2k+3k+4k+90 = 360 → 9k = 270 → k = 30. Angles: 60°, 90°, 120°. Largest = 120°.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a trapezium with AB ∥ CD. ∠A = 55° and ∠B = 70°. What are ∠C and ∠D?
In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. E is the midpoint of AB and F is the midpoint of DC. Which of the following is true about AEFD?
questions.explanation: AE = DF (half of equal opposite sides AB and DC). AE ∥ DF (since AB ∥ DC). So AEFD is a parallelogram.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a square of side 5 cm. What is the length of diagonal AC?
questions.explanation: In square ABCD, angle B = 90°. AC² = AB² + BC² = 25 + 25 = 50. AC = √50 = 5√2 cm.

questions.text: Each interior angle of a regular polygon is 150°. How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = 180° − 150° = 30°. Number of sides = 360° ÷ 30° = 12.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a rhombus. ∠DAB = 60°. What is ∠DBC?
questions.explanation: In rhombus ABCD, ∠DAB=60°. Adjacent angles supplementary: ∠ABC=120°. Diagonal BD bisects ∠B. ∠DBC = ∠ABC/2 = 60°.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. P is a point on BC such that BP:PC = 1:2. DP extended meets AB extended at Q. If DC = 9 cm, what is QB?
questions.explanation: In △DPC and △QPB: ∠DCP=∠QBP (alternate, DC∥AB), ∠DPC=∠QPB (vertical). Triangles similar. PC/BP = DC/QB → 2/1 = 9/QB → QB = 4.5 cm.

questions.text: ABCD is a parallelogram with AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm and ∠ABC = 60°. What is the length of the shorter diagonal?
questions.explanation: Using the law of cosines in △ABC (diagonal AC is shorter since ∠ABC=60°<90°): AC² = AB²+BC²−2(AB)(BC)cos60° = 49+25−2(7)(5)(0.5) = 74−35 = 39. AC = √39 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a rectangle. Diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. If ∠AOB = 70°, what is ∠OAB?
questions.explanation: In rectangle, diagonals are equal and bisect each other. OA=OB (halves of equal diagonals). △OAB is isosceles. ∠AOB=70°. ∠OAB=∠OBA=(180°−70°)/2=55°.

questions.text: In the figure, the diagonals of rhombus PQRS intersect at O. OA ⊥ PQ where A is on PQ. If ∠QPR = 40°, what is ∠PQR?
questions.explanation: In rhombus, diagonal bisects the vertex angle. ∠QPR=40° means diagonal PR bisects ∠P, so ∠QPR=∠SPR=40° → ∠P=80°. Adjacent angles supplementary: ∠PQR=180°−80°=100°.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral where ∠DAB = 90°, AB = 15 cm, BC = 17 cm, CD = 5 cm and DA = 20 cm (right angle at A only, not a rectangle). What is BD?
questions.explanation: In △ABD, ∠DAB=90°. BD²=AB²+DA²=225+400=625. BD=25 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram with AE ⊥ DC and CF ⊥ AD. If AB = 10 cm, AE = 6 cm and CF = 8 cm, what is AD?
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height. Using AB as base: Area = AB × AE = 10 × 6 = 60 cm². Using AD as base: Area = AD × CF → 60 = AD × 8 → AD = 7.5 cm.

D.label: Numerical — Figure Based
D.geometry_note: All MCQ — geometry chapter. Student identifies correct answer from options.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. ∠DAB = (2x + 15)° and ∠BCD = (3x − 10)°. Find ∠ABC.
In rhombus ABCD, diagonal AC = 16 cm and side AB = 10 cm. What is the length of diagonal BD?
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect at right angles. AO = 8 cm. In right △AOB: BO² = AB²−AO² = 100−64 = 36. BO = 6 cm. BD = 2×6 = 12 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, PQRS is a parallelogram. If ∠PSR = 125°, what is ∠PQR + ∠QRS?
questions.explanation: In parallelogram PQRS: ∠PQR=∠PSR=125° (opposite angles). ∠QRS=180°-125°=55° (adjacent supplementary). Sum=125°+55°=180°.

questions.text: In rectangle ABCD, the diagonal AC = 13 cm and AB = 5 cm. What is BC?
questions.explanation: In rectangle, all angles = 90°. AC² = AB² + BC². 169 = 25 + BC². BC² = 144. BC = 12 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a trapezium with AB ∥ CD. AB = 14 cm, CD = 8 cm and the height h = 6 cm. What is the area of the trapezium?
questions.explanation: Area of trapezium = ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height = ½ × (14+8) × 6 = ½ × 22 × 6 = 66 cm².

questions.text: In a parallelogram ABCD, the diagonals are AC = 24 cm and BD = 20 cm. They intersect at O. What is OA + OB?
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other. OA = AC/2 = 12 cm. OB = BD/2 = 10 cm. OA+OB = 22 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a square. The diagonals intersect at O. If AO = 6 cm, what is the area of the square?
questions.explanation: AO=6 cm → diagonal AC = 12 cm. In square with diagonal d: side = d/√2 = 12/√2. Area = side² = (12/√2)² = 144/2 = 72 cm².

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a regular hexagon with side 6 cm. What is the sum of interior angles? What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: Sum = (6−2)×180° = 720°. Regular hexagon: each angle = 720°/6 = 120°.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. The area is 48 cm². If AB = 8 cm, what is the perpendicular height from D to AB?
questions.explanation: Area = base × height. 48 = 8 × h. h = 6 cm.

questions.text: In rhombus ABCD, ∠A = 60°. What is ∠ABD (the angle that diagonal BD makes with side AB)?
questions.explanation: In rhombus ABCD with ∠A=60°: AB=AD, so △ABD is isosceles. ∠DAB=60° and AB=AD means △ABD is equilateral. ∠ABD=60°.

cases.case_text: A farmer has a field in the shape of a parallelogram ABCD with AB = 120 m, BC = 80 m and ∠ABC = 60°. He wants to divide the field using a fence along diagonal BD. He also plants trees at each vertex.

subparts.explanation: In parallelogram, opposite angles are equal. ∠ADC = ∠ABC = 60°.
Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary. ∠DAB = 180° − ∠ABC = 180° − 60° = 120°.

subparts.text: The perimeter of the field is:

cases.case_text: In a geometry class, a teacher draws quadrilateral ABCD on the board with the following measurements: AB = 6 cm, BC = 8 cm, CD = 6 cm, DA = 8 cm, and diagonal AC = 10 cm. She asks students questions about the figure.

subparts.text: What type of quadrilateral is ABCD? (AB = CD and BC = DA, with diagonal AC = 10 cm)
subparts.explanation: AB=CD=6 cm, BC=DA=8 cm (opposite sides equal) → parallelogram. Check ∠ABC: AB²+BC²=36+64=100=AC²=10². By Pythagoras converse, ∠ABC=90°. So ABCD is a rectangle.

subparts.text: In △ABC with AB = 6 cm, BC = 8 cm, AC = 10 cm. What is ∠ABC?
subparts.explanation: AB²+BC² = 36+64 = 100 = AC². By the converse of Pythagoras theorem, ∠ABC = 90°.

subparts.text: What is the area of the quadrilateral ABCD (rectangle with sides 6 cm and 8 cm)?
subparts.explanation: Area of rectangle = length × breadth = 8 × 6 = 48 cm².
